description: 'sihewa' selects light wind, felt as a modest movement of air against skin, leaves, cloth, or water. It may cool a warm place, though a cold breeze can make the same place less comfortable. 'howeli' (wind) covers the full range of ordinary moving air.
articulatory_notes: Narrow the airstream along the centre of the tongue for unvoiced 's' and move into high-front 'i'. Open the glottis for 'h' and let the breath enter stressed middle-height front 'e'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for the 'w' glide, then open into central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: No complete closure interrupts the word. Its sibilant, open glottal breath, and final glide keep a thin stream of air moving through all three syllables, which suits a light wind as an embodied mnemonic.

semantic_domains.nature: a light movement of air within local weather
semantic_domains.physical: moving air felt gently against a surface or body
